rulings.Case Title: THIRD AND MISSION ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. PETER W. CHIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 2, 2014, line 4. PLAINTIFF THIRD AND MISSION ASSOCIATES' Notice of Hearing for Claim Of Exemption; DENIED. rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. LESZEK GATARZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 2, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT LESZEK GATARZ's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b) and show that the judgment was entered/stay was lifted due to mistake, surprise of excusable neglect. Additionally, Declaration of Mary Gillespie paragraph 4 does not assert that Defendants complied with the terms of the stipulation, it merely recites the terms of the stipulation which provided for stay of judgment as long as Defendants complied with the terms of the stipulation. Gillespie Declaration 8:5. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARY COURT PROPERTIES LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY VS. WADE TREVOR WALKER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 2, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT HOURI PARSI's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT DENIED in part and OFF CALENDAR in part. Motion is OFF CALENDAR as to Defendant Walker, untimely per CCP 1005. Order Shortening Time was not sought and was not granted as to Defendant Walker. OST application was made by Tom Elke, then counsel for Parsi only. Motion as to Defendant Parsi is denied. Defendant was served with the notice of Order Striking her answer on March 5, 2014. Defendant failed to address March 5, 2014 order until November 13, 2014. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: XELAN PROP I LLC VS. MARIA MARANGHI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 2, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT MARTHA BINI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges grounds for unlawful detainer re: nuisance. Complaint is not uncertain. Whether or not some of the alleged breaches are nonsensical is an issue of fact not properly raised on demurrer. =(501/REQ)
